prompt: Our team of 4 backend engineers has been working on an internal data pipeline tool for 6 months. We've been pretty fast and loose — no branch protection, no CI, everyone just pushes to main. We've had a few incidents where a bad merge broke the daily ETL job and we had to scramble to fix it at 6am. Python 3.11. How do we get this under control?

assertions.text: Output recommends a named tier (Minimal, Standard, or Strict)
Tier is Standard or Strict — 4-person team with production incidents warrants escalated governance
Branch protection is highlighted as the most urgent or highest-priority fix (not buried at the end)
Output includes CI GitHub Actions workflow snippet
Output includes pre-commit hook config with black and ruff
Output explains WHY items matter in terms of preventing the type of incident described (bad merge → 6am scramble)
Output includes effort estimates for items
